LanguageData.UWU_CustomizeGizmoDesc: Select a colonist to customize this weapon at a nearby workbench.
LanguageData.UWU_RelicNameTooltip: This weapon is an ideology relic. Its name can only be changed when forming or reforming an ideoligion.
LanguageData.UWU_SelectTraitFirst: Select at least one trait first
LanguageData.UWU_NotEnoughMaterials: Not enough materials on the map
LanguageData.UWU_InsufficientResources: Not enough resources to customize this weapon.
LanguageData.UWU_IngredientShortfall: {0} customization interrupted: ingredients lost before {1} could be applied.
LanguageData.UWU_BailWorkbenchUnavailable: {0} customization interrupted: workbench is no longer available.
LanguageData.UWU_BailWorkbenchInactive: {0} customization interrupted: workbench has no power or fuel.
LanguageData.UWU_BailWeaponInaccessible: {0} customization interrupted: weapon is no longer accessible.
LanguageData.UWU_BailIngredientLost: {0} customization interrupted: an ingredient was lost while hauling.
LanguageData.UWU_RelicIdeoColorTip: {0}'s ideoligion color. This weapon is a relic of the {1} ideoligion.
LanguageData.UWU_RefundRateNoEffect: Trait refund rate has no effect while trait cost multiplier is 0%.
LanguageData.UWU_UseRecipeBaseCost: Derive base cost from crafting recipe
LanguageData.UWU_UseRecipeBaseCostDesc: When enabled, the base material cost for adding traits is derived from the weapon's crafting recipe. When disabled, a flat cost based on generic resources at the weapon's tech level are used instead. Disabling this prevents cheap-to-craft weapons from having unreasonably low trait costs, but may also prevent distinct/modded weapons from having appropriately thematic costs.
LanguageData.UWU_RequireCustomizationResearchDesc: Require the completion of the Unique Smithing, Unique Machining, or Unique Fabrication research projects before customizing weapons of the corresponding tech level. When disabled, these research projects are hidden from the research tree.
LanguageData.UWU_RequireRecipeResearchDesc: Require the completion of any research that would enable crafting the weapon. For example, customizing a charge rifle would require Pulse-charged munitions.
LanguageData.UWU_RequireWorkbenchDesc: Require that the workbench matches the weapon's tech level. When disabled, any workbench that can craft weapons is sufficient for customizing all weapons.
LanguageData.UWU_AllowUncraftableDesc: Allow customization of weapons that have no crafting recipe.
LanguageData.UWU_AllowUltratechDesc: Allow customization of ultratech weapons, requiring Advanced Weapon Customization research.
LanguageData.UWU_AllowArchotechDesc: Allow customization of archotech weapons, requiring Advanced Weapon Customization research.
LanguageData.UWU_AllowDefConversionDesc: Allow weapons to convert between base and unique defs during customization. When disabled, only already-unique weapons can be customized, and removing all traits keeps the weapon as a zero-trait unique instead of reverting to its base weapon.
LanguageData.UWU_EnableIdeoColorsDesc: Show Ideology and miscellaneous color palettes in the weapon customization color picker.
LanguageData.UWU_EnableStructureColorsDesc: Show structure color palette in the weapon customization color picker.
LanguageData.UWU_EnforceMaxTraitLimitDesc: Vanilla unique weapons generate with no more than three traits. Enable this to enforce the same limit during customization. Disable to allow any number of traits (other rules such as category and exclusion restrictions still apply).
LanguageData.UWU_EnforceSoleTraitDesc: Some traits cannot be the only trait on a weapon during vanilla generation. Enable this to enforce the same restriction during customization.
LanguageData.UWU_EnableGroundCustomizationDesc: Show "Customize" options when right-clicking weapons on the ground. Disable this to reduce float menu clutter, especially with storage mods that stack items in the same cell. Weapons can still be customized by selecting them and using the gizmo button.
